title: Handle a Dispatch Exception
tagline: Resolve a situation where a driver's van broke down halfway through their route.
scenario: You are the Dispatch Manager for a courier company. Driver A's van has broken down on the N1 highway. They have 15 parcels left on board, 3 of which are medical supplies with a strict 4 PM delivery deadline. It is currently 1 PM.
brief: Formulate a recovery plan. You have one backup driver (Driver B) currently 30 minutes away, but pulling them off their route will delay 10 non-urgent residential deliveries.
deliverables: Your immediate action plan (who is doing what right now)
The communication sent to Driver A and Driver B
The triage decision: which parcels get rescued by Driver B, and which stay in the broken van for the tow truck
The SMS sent to the 10 customers whose residential deliveries are now delayed
submissionGuidance: Exception handling is about triage. You cannot save every SLA. Protect the medical supplies, sacrifice the residential deliveries, and over-communicate.

rubric.label: Triage Decision
rubric.description: Did you correctly prioritise the medical supplies over residential?

rubric.label: Action Plan
rubric.description: Is the physical logistics plan (Driver B rescue) realistic?

rubric.label: Delay Communication
rubric.description: Is the delay message to the impacted customers apologetic but firm?
